source.title: State of Texas v. PDD Holdings Inc. / WhaleCo Inc. d/b/a Temu — AG complaint

extractions.claim: Texas AG sued PDD Holdings and WhaleCo Inc. (Temu) in February 2026 for 'spyware disguised as a shopping app'
extractions.evidence: Texas AG Ken Paxton filed suit against PDD Holdings Inc. and WhaleCo Inc. (d/b/a Temu) alleging unlawful deception and covert data harvesting. The app allegedly requests permissions for device logs, external storage, installation packages, and precise location data without clear disclosure. Paxton characterized Temu as a 'trojan horse' capable of bypassing security protocols.

verification.verifier_notes: Complaint document available on Courthouse News. Covered by Bloomberg Law, Law360, Yahoo News. Filed as part of 4 anti-CCP lawsuits in 3 days.

extractions.claim: Texas DTPA penalties: up to $10,000 per violation, $250,000 per violation targeting consumers 65+
extractions.evidence: Lawsuit seeks relief under Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act (DTPA) with up to $10,000 per violation and up to $250,000 per violation when targeting consumers 65+. Also alleges deceptive marketing — showing fake discounted prices when the 'discounted' price is actually market price.

verification.verifier_notes: DTPA penalty structure is statutory. Per-violation structure could theoretically scale to large amounts but enforcement typically results in settlements.

extractions.claim: Lawsuit references Google's 2023 suspension of Pinduoduo app for malware as precedent for Temu security concerns
extractions.evidence: The complaint cites Google's 2023 suspension of Temu's sister app, Pinduoduo, after malware was discovered in its code. This is cited as a 'troubling precedent' for Temu's operations.

verification.verifier_notes: Google did temporarily suspend Pinduoduo from Play Store in March 2023 over security concerns. This is historical fact used as supporting evidence in the complaint.
